data.body:
[llm-prompts overlay / Pass-1] Promptâ†”schema DRIFT: the diff adds `severity` to the `required` array of the `code_review_findings` output schema, but every code-review finder that declares `outputs: code_review_findings` explicitly instructs the model NOT to emit severity, and the bound contract model has no `severity` field.

Evidence:
- Contract model: src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py:32-54 `_code_finding_model()` defines CodeFinding with fields finding/criteria/location/evidence/scenarios/impact/checklist_item/suggested_fix â€” NO `severity`. Registered as `code_review_findings` at contracts.py:120.
- Prompt instruction (all finders): src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md:39 "Do NOT emit severity, confidence, or priority â€” a later pass computes those deterministically." and front-matter code-review-base.md:7 "No model-emitted severity (computed deterministically in Pass 3)." Mirrored in code-review-tests.md:73, code-review-security.md, code-review-llm-prompts.md:68, etc.
- Making `severity` REQUIRED means every conformant emission (which omits severity per prompt instruction) fails schema validation â€” a required key the prompt never produces.
- This is the exact shape of seeded regression `R-schema-key-required-not-emitted` (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml:55-68).
